items.scenario: You signed up for a weekend self-defense class run by the campus recreation center. The session was moved to a smaller room without notice, and the equipment you were promised was unavailable.
items.direction: Write an email to the recreation center coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what happened during the relocated session
Explain how the missing equipment affected the class
Suggest a way to handle room changes in the future
items.subject: Concerns about the weekend self-defense class

items.scenario: You are a second-year student who missed two weeks of a statistics course because of a documented illness. The midterm is scheduled for next Monday and you feel unprepared.
items.direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you fell behind in the course
Ask whether the midterm date could be adjusted
Mention the materials you still need to review
items.subject: Request regarding the upcoming statistics midterm

items.scenario: You are a finalist for a summer marketing internship and were asked to confirm your start date. A scheduling conflict with your graduation ceremony means you cannot begin on the proposed day.
items.direction: Write an email to the hiring manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conflict with the proposed start date
Suggest an alternative week to begin the internship
Offer to complete any onboarding tasks remotely beforehand
items.subject: Confirming my internship start date

items.scenario: You are a tenant in a campus apartment where the hot water has been intermittent for the past week. Two repair visits were scheduled but no technician showed up.
items.direction: Write an email to the building manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the hot water
Explain how the missed repair visits have affected you
Ask when a technician can realistically be expected
items.subject: Ongoing hot water issue in my apartment

items.scenario: You are the secretary of the campus photography club, which is planning an outdoor exhibition next month. You need to reserve the courtyard space and arrange display boards.
items.direction: Write an email to the student activities office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the exhibition the club is planning
Ask about reserving the courtyard for the event
Suggest a date when display boards could be delivered
items.subject: Reserving space for the photography exhibition

items.scenario: You recently used the campus printing service to produce posters for a fundraiser, but half of them came out with faded colors. You picked them up the evening before the event.
items.direction: Write an email to the print shop supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the printed posters
Explain why the timing made the issue difficult
Ask whether a reprint or refund is possible
items.subject: Quality issue with my recent poster order

items.scenario: You are a graduate student who would like to switch from the morning lab section to the afternoon one because of a conflict with a teaching assistant duty. Enrollment closes Friday.
items.direction: Write an email to the lab instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you need to change lab sections
Ask whether a spot is available in the afternoon section
Provide the details of your current enrollment
items.subject: Request to switch lab sections

items.scenario: You completed a two-week trial shift at the campus library help desk and were told you would hear back about a permanent position. A week has passed with no update.
items.direction: Write an email to the library supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention the trial shifts you recently completed
Ask about the status of the permanent position
Explain that you remain very interested in the role
items.subject: Following up on the help desk position
